# runtime/semantos-brain/tests/oddjobz_derivations_parity_conformance.zig

```zig
// D-DOG.1.0c Phase 2B.4 — Zig-side parity-oracle test for the
// oddjobz cell-derivation surface.
//
// Reference fixture: core/cell-ops/tests/vectors/oddjobz-derivations.json
//   (generated by core/cell-ops/tests/vectors/generate_oddjobz_derivations.ts;
//    consumed unchanged by both this Zig test and the TS-side test
//    `core/cell-ops/src/__tests__/oddjobz-derivations.test.ts`).
//
// The fixture's `expected.*` fields are stamped from the TS
// implementation. This test loads the SAME file, drives every case
// through the Zig implementation in `oddjobz_derivations.zig`, and
// asserts the output equals the stamped value. If TS and Zig produce
// divergent output for the same input, this test fails — that's the
// cross-language byte-equality oracle Phase 2B.4 is built to enforce.
//
// Three derivations covered:
//
//   1. `normaliseAddress` + `deriveLookupKey` — address canonicalisation.
//   2. `uuidV5LikeFromBytes` — 32-byte cellID → 32-char UUID-shape hex.
//   3. cellID hashes for site / customer / job / attachment.

const std = @import("std");
const oddjobz_derivations = @import("oddjobz_derivations");

// ─── Fixture loader ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

/// Walk up from cwd to find the fixture. Mirrors the BKDS / cell-
/// signing fixture lookup pattern (`zig build test` runs with cwd =
/// `runtime/semantos-brain`; the fixture lives under `core/cell-ops/tests/`).
fn readFixture(allocator: std.mem.Allocator) ![]u8 {
    const candidates = [_][]const u8{
        "../../core/cell-ops/tests/vectors/oddjobz-derivations.json",
        "../core/cell-ops/tests/vectors/oddjobz-derivations.json",
        "core/cell-ops/tests/vectors/oddjobz-derivations.json",
        "../../../core/cell-ops/tests/vectors/oddjobz-derivations.json",
    };
    for (candidates) |c| {
        const f = std.fs.cwd().openFile(c, .{}) catch continue;
        defer f.close();
        return try f.readToEndAlloc(allocator, 1 * 1024 * 1024);
    }
    return error.fixture_not_found;
}

// ─── Hex helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────

fn decodeHex32(hex: []const u8) ![32]u8 {
    if (hex.len != 64) return error.bad_hex_length;
    var out: [32]u8 = undefined;
    for (&out, 0..) |*b, i| {
        const hi = try std.fmt.charToDigit(hex[i * 2], 16);
        const lo = try std.fmt.charToDigit(hex[i * 2 + 1], 16);
        b.* = @intCast(hi * 16 + lo);
    }
    return out;
}

fn encodeHex32(bytes: [32]u8) [64]u8 {
    return std.fmt.bytesToHex(bytes, .lower);
}

// ─── JSON case extractors ─────────────────────────────────────────────
//
// Cases share `.kind`, `.name`, `.input`, `.expected` keys but their
// inner shapes differ. We dispatch on `.kind` and pull the strings
// directly via `std.json.Value` rather than parseInto, so the test
// stays independent of the Zig struct layout (and rejects mis-typed
// fixtures with a clear error).

fn getString(obj: std.json.Value, key: []const u8) ![]const u8 {
    const o = obj.object;
    const v = o.get(key) orelse return error.missing_field;
    return switch (v) {
        .string => |s| s,
        else => error.field_not_string,
    };
}

fn getOptionalString(obj: std.json.Value, key: []const u8) !?[]const u8 {
    const o = obj.object;
    const v = o.get(key) orelse return null;
    return switch (v) {
        .string => |s| s,
        .null => null,
        else => error.field_wrong_type,
    };
}

fn getBool(obj: std.json.Value, key: []const u8) !bool {
    const o = obj.object;
    const v = o.get(key) orelse return error.missing_field;
    return switch (v) {
        .bool => |b| b,
        else => error.field_not_bool,
    };
}

fn getArray(obj: std.json.Value, key: []const u8) ![]std.json.Value {
    const o = obj.object;
    const v = o.get(key) orelse return error.missing_field;
    return switch (v) {
        .array => |a| a.items,
        else => error.field_not_array,
    };
}

fn getObject(obj: std.json.Value, key: []const u8) !std.json.Value {
    const o = obj.object;
    const v = o.get(key) orelse return error.missing_field;
    if (v != .object) return error.field_not_object;
    return v;
}

// ─── Per-kind verifiers ───────────────────────────────────────────────

fn verifyAddress(allocator: std.mem.Allocator, case: std.json.Value, name: []const u8) !void {
    const input = try getObject(case, "input");
    const expected = try getObject(case, "expected");
    const address = try getString(input, "address");
    const key_number = try getOptionalString(input, "keyNumber");
    const expected_normalised = try getString(expected, "normalisedAddress");
    const expected_lookup = try getString(expected, "lookupKey");

    const got_normalised = try oddjobz_derivations.normaliseAddress(allocator, address);
    defer allocator.free(got_normalised);
    if (!std.mem.eql(u8, got_normalised, expected_normalised)) {
        std.debug.print(
            "PARITY FAIL [address|{s}]: normalised\n  expected: {s}\n  got:      {s}\n",
            .{ name, expected_normalised, got_normalised },
        );
        return error.normalised_mismatch;
    }

    const got_lookup = try oddjobz_derivations.deriveLookupKey(allocator, got_normalised, key_number);
    defer allocator.free(got_lookup);
    if (!std.mem.eql(u8, got_lookup, expected_lookup)) {
        std.debug.print(
            "PARITY FAIL [address|{s}]: lookupKey\n  expected: {s}\n  got:      {s}\n",
            .{ name, expected_lookup, got_lookup },
        );
        return error.lookup_key_mismatch;
    }
}

fn verifyUuid(allocator: std.mem.Allocator, case: std.json.Value, name: []const u8) !void {
    const input = try getObject(case, "input");
    const expected = try getObject(case, "expected");
    const bytes_hex = try getString(input, "bytes32Hex");
    const expected_uuid = try getString(expected, "uuidHex");

    const src = try decodeHex32(bytes_hex);
    const got = try oddjobz_derivations.uuidV5LikeFromBytes(allocator, &src);
    defer allocator.free(got);
    if (!std.mem.eql(u8, got, expected_uuid)) {
        std.debug.print(
            "PARITY FAIL [uuid|{s}]:\n  expected: {s}\n  got:      {s}\n",
            .{ name, expected_uuid, got },
        );
        return error.uuid_mismatch;
    }
    // Structural sanity: 32 hex chars; version nibble at index 12 = '4';
    // variant high two bits at index 16 ∈ {8,9,a,b}.
    try std.testing.expectEqual(@as(usize, 32), got.len);
    try std.testing.expectEqual(@as(u8, '4'), got[12]);
    const variant_char = got[16];
    try std.testing.expect(variant_char == '8' or variant_char == '9' or variant_char == 'a' or variant_char == 'b');
}

fn verifySiteCellId(case: std.json.Value, name: []const u8) !void {
    const input = try getObject(case, "input");
    const expected = try getObject(case, "expected");
    const normalised = try getString(input, "normalisedAddress");
    const key_number = try getOptionalString(input, "keyNumber");
    const full = try getString(input, "fullAddress");
    const expected_hex = try getString(expected, "cellIdHex");

    const cell_id = oddjobz_derivations.computeSiteCellId(normalised, key_number, full);
    const got_hex = encodeHex32(cell_id);
    if (!std.mem.eql(u8, got_hex[0..], expected_hex)) {
        std.debug.print(
            "PARITY FAIL [site_cell_id|{s}]:\n  expected: {s}\n  got:      {s}\n",
            .{ name, expected_hex, got_hex },
        );
        return error.site_cell_id_mismatch;
    }
}

fn verifyCustomerCellId(case: std.json.Value, name: []const u8) !void {
    const input = try getObject(case, "input");
    const expected = try getObject(case, "expected");
    const cust_name = try getString(input, "name");
    const role = try getString(input, "role");
    const site_hex = try getString(input, "siteCellIdHex");
    const phone = try getString(input, "phone");
    const email = try getString(input, "email");
    const expected_hex = try getString(expected, "cellIdHex");

    const site_bytes = try decodeHex32(site_hex);
    const cell_id = oddjobz_derivations.computeCustomerCellId(cust_name, role, site_bytes, phone, email);
    const got_hex = encodeHex32(cell_id);
    if (!std.mem.eql(u8, got_hex[0..], expected_hex)) {
        std.debug.print(
            "PARITY FAIL [customer_cell_id|{s}]:\n  expected: {s}\n  got:      {s}\n",
            .{ name, expected_hex, got_hex },
        );
        return error.customer_cell_id_mismatch;
    }
}

fn verifyJobCellId(allocator: std.mem.Allocator, case: std.json.Value, name: []const u8) !void {
    const input = try getObject(case, "input");
    const expected = try getObject(case, "expected");
    const site_hex = try getString(input, "siteCellIdHex");
    const refs_arr = try getArray(input, "customerRefs");
    const work_order = try getString(input, "workOrderNumber");
    const issuance = try getString(input, "issuanceDate");
    const due = try getString(input, "dueDate");
    const display_name = try getString(input, "displayName");
    const created_at = try getString(input, "createdAt");
    const expected_hex = try getString(expected, "cellIdHex");

    const site_bytes = try decodeHex32(site_hex);

    const refs = try allocator.alloc(oddjobz_derivations.CustomerRefHash, refs_arr.len);
    defer allocator.free(refs);
    for (refs_arr, 0..) |ref_v, i| {
        const ref_hex = try getString(ref_v, "cellIdHex");
        const role = try getString(ref_v, "role");
        const primary = try getBool(ref_v, "primary");
        refs[i] = .{
            .cell_id = try decodeHex32(ref_hex),
            .role = role,
            .primary = primary,
        };
    }

    const cell_id = oddjobz_derivations.computeJobCellId(
        site_bytes,
        refs,
        work_order,
        issuance,
        due,
        created_at,
        display_name,
    );
    const got_hex = encodeHex32(cell_id);
    if (!std.mem.eql(u8, got_hex[0..], expected_hex)) {
        std.debug.print(
            "PARITY FAIL [job_cell_id|{s}]:\n  expected: {s}\n  got:      {s}\n",
            .{ name, expected_hex, got_hex },
        );
        return error.job_cell_id_mismatch;
    }
}

fn verifyAttachmentCellId(case: std.json.Value, name: []const u8) !void {
    const input = try getObject(case, "input");
    const expected = try getObject(case, "expected");
    const job_hex = try getString(input, "jobCellIdHex");
    const source_path = try getString(input, "sourceAttachmentPath");
    const created_at = try getString(input, "createdAt");
    const expected_hex = try getString(expected, "cellIdHex");

    const job_bytes = try decodeHex32(job_hex);
    const cell_id = oddjobz_derivations.computeAttachmentCellId(job_bytes, source_path, created_at);
    const got_hex = encodeHex32(cell_id);
    if (!std.mem.eql(u8, got_hex[0..], expected_hex)) {
        std.debug.print(
            "PARITY FAIL [attachment_cell_id|{s}]:\n  expected: {s}\n  got:      {s}\n",
            .{ name, expected_hex, got_hex },
        );
        return error.attachment_cell_id_mismatch;
    }
}

// ─── Test entry points ────────────────────────────────────────────────

test "D-DOG.1.0c Phase 2B.4 oddjobz_derivations parity: every fixture case round-trips byte-equal" {
    const allocator = std.testing.allocator;

    const raw = try readFixture(allocator);
    defer allocator.free(raw);

    var parsed = try std.json.parseFromSlice(std.json.Value, allocator, raw, .{});
    defer parsed.deinit();

    const root = parsed.value;
    if (root != .object) return error.fixture_root_not_object;

    const version_v = root.object.get("version") orelse return error.missing_version;
    if (version_v != .integer or version_v.integer != 1) {
        std.debug.print(
            "fixture version mismatch: expected 1, got {any}\n",
            .{version_v},
        );
        return error.unexpected_fixture_version;
    }

    const cases_v = root.object.get("cases") orelse return error.missing_cases;
    if (cases_v != .array) return error.cases_not_array;
    const cases = cases_v.array.items;

    // Per-kind counters so the test fails loudly if a fixture
    // regenerator drops a kind by accident.
    var n_address: usize = 0;
    var n_uuid: usize = 0;
    var n_site: usize = 0;
    var n_customer: usize = 0;
    var n_job: usize = 0;
    var n_attachment: usize = 0;

    for (cases) |c| {
        if (c != .object) return error.case_not_object;
        const kind = try getString(c, "kind");
        const name = try getString(c, "name");
        if (std.mem.eql(u8, kind, "address")) {
            try verifyAddress(allocator, c, name);
            n_address += 1;
        } else if (std.mem.eql(u8, kind, "uuid")) {
            try verifyUuid(allocator, c, name);
            n_uuid += 1;
        } else if (std.mem.eql(u8, kind, "site_cell_id")) {
            try verifySiteCellId(c, name);
            n_site += 1;
        } else if (std.mem.eql(u8, kind, "customer_cell_id")) {
            try verifyCustomerCellId(c, name);
            n_customer += 1;
        } else if (std.mem.eql(u8, kind, "job_cell_id")) {
            try verifyJobCellId(allocator, c, name);
            n_job += 1;
        } else if (std.mem.eql(u8, kind, "attachment_cell_id")) {
            try verifyAttachmentCellId(c, name);
            n_attachment += 1;
        } else {
            std.debug.print("unknown fixture kind: {s}\n", .{kind});
            return error.unknown_kind;
        }
    }

    // Floors mirror the TS test's "at least N per kind" assertions.
    try std.testing.expect(n_address >= 5);
    try std.testing.expect(n_uuid >= 3);
    try std.testing.expect(n_site >= 3);
    try std.testing.expect(n_customer >= 3);
    try std.testing.expect(n_job >= 3);
    try std.testing.expect(n_attachment >= 3);
    try std.testing.expect(cases.len >= 20);
}

```
